tasks.title: Split require_repoprompt config into plan_required and tool_injection flags with iteration-aware behavior
evidence: crates/ralph/src/contracts.rs:108: require_repoprompt is a single Option<bool> field in AgentConfig.
docs/configuration.md:28: require_repoprompt is documented as a single boolean flag.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs:286-334: Multiple iterations are handled in a loop, with is_followup = iteration_index > 1.
crates/ralph/src/promptflow.rs:92-112: build_phase1_prompt receives policy.require_repoprompt and passes it to render_worker_phase1_prompt.
User request: Separate into 'repoprompt_plan_required' (injects context_builder planning instructions) and 'repoprompt_tool_injection' (only injects tooling reminders).

User request: When multiple iterations are enabled, do NOT honor 'repoprompt_plan_required' config for phase 1 of subsequent iterations (only include it in the first iteration). DO honor 'repoprompt_tool_injection' config for all iterations.
plan: Add two new fields to AgentConfig in contracts.rs: repoprompt_plan_required (Option<bool>) and repoprompt_tool_injection (Option<bool>).
Update AgentConfig::merge_from to merge the new fields.
Update Config::default() in contracts.rs to set appropriate defaults for both new fields.
Update the schema (schemas/config.schema.json) to include the two new fields.
Update PromptPolicy in promptflow.rs to have separate fields: repoprompt_plan_required and repoprompt_tool_injection.
Update run_cmd.rs to create PromptPolicy with both flags, checking iteration context (is_followup) when building phase 1 prompts.

Modify build_phase1_prompt in promptflow.rs to accept iteration context (is_followup) and conditionally exclude repoprompt_plan_required for followup iterations while always including repoprompt_tool_injection.
Update execute_phase1_planning in phases.rs to pass is_followup context to build_phase1_prompt.

Find all other usages of require_repoprompt in promptflow.rs and run_cmd/phases.rs and update logic to use the new flags appropriately (repoprompt_tool_injection for all phases, repoprompt_plan_required only for first iteration phase 1).
Add tests covering: both flags true, only plan_required true, only tool_injection true, both false, and iteration-specific behavior (plan_required excluded in followup iterations).
Update docs/configuration.md to document the new separate flags and iteration-specific behavior.
Deprecate but keep require_repoprompt for backward compatibility, mapping it to repoprompt_plan_required for migration.
tasks.request: Separate the repoprompt config settings into 2 configuration settings instead of 1. One config setting for 'repoprompt_plan_required' and one for 'repoprompt_tool_injection'. The first config injects the context_builder planning instructions while the latter only injects the repo prompt tooling reminders. When multiple iterations are enabled, do NOT honor the 'repoprompt_plan_required' config for phase 1 of the subsequent iterations. Only include it in the first iteration. DO honor the 'repoprompt_tool_injection' config for all iterations. Phase 1 of the subsequent iterations will still plan, just don't include the mandatory instructions to use the repo prompt context_builder.

tasks.title: Use porcelain -z parsing for git status to handle spaces/renames safely
evidence: crates/ralph/src/gitutil.rs: status_porcelain runs `git status --porcelain` and parse_status_path splits on ' -> '.
crates/ralph/src/gitutil.rs: parse_status_path relies on whitespace-delimited lines, which is brittle for quoted paths.
plan: Switch to `git status --porcelain -z` and parse NUL-delimited entries (including renames).
Update status_paths/parse_status_path logic to handle rename pairs and spaces reliably.
Add tests for paths with spaces and rename output.

tasks.title: Make gitutil upstream tests offline-safe (no external git push)
evidence: crates/ralph/tests/gitutil_test.rs: test_upstream_ref_with_upstream pushes to https://github.com/test/test.git and notes it may fail.
plan: Replace external remote with a local bare repo for upstream setup.
Update the test to set upstream without network access.
Verify tests pass in offline/CI environments.

tasks.title: Align queue schema required fields with runtime validation
evidence: schemas/queue.schema.json: Task.required lists only id and title.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: validate_task_required_fields errors on missing created_at/updated_at.
docs/queue-and-tasks.md: required field list includes created_at/updated_at/tags/scope/evidence/plan/notes.
plan: Update queue.schema.json required list to match validation requirements.
Adjust schema alignment tests (e.g., schema_alignment_test.rs) if they assert the old schema.
Confirm docs/queue-and-tasks.md matches the updated schema and validation behavior.

tasks.title: Enforce done.json to contain only done/rejected tasks
evidence: .ralph/README.md: done.json is described as the archive of completed tasks.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: validate_queue_set uses validate_queue on done without enforcing terminal status.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/archive.rs: archive only moves done/rejected into done.json (implies terminal-only).
plan: Add validation for done.json to allow only done/rejected statuses.
Add tests for invalid done.json statuses and ensure queue validate fails appropriately.
Update docs/README if wording needs to reflect the enforcement.

tasks.title: Remove install side effects from make ci or gate it
evidence: Makefile: ci target includes install.
Makefile: install writes to ~/.local/bin and runs the binary, causing side effects in CI.
plan: Move install out of make ci or add a guard/flag to skip it in CI.
Update docs to reflect the new ci behavior.
Verify make ci still runs the full quality gate without writing to user bin.

tasks.title: Expand make clean to remove repo temp/lock artifacts
evidence: Makefile: clean only runs cargo clean and deletes *.log files.
Repo creates .ralph/cache and .ralph/lock directories that are not removed by clean.
plan: Extend make clean to remove .ralph/cache, .ralph/lock, and other temp artifacts.
Ensure clean remains safe (do not delete user config or secrets).
Update any docs if clean behavior changes.

tasks.title: Allow require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths to ignore directory prefixes
evidence: crates/ralph/src/gitutil.rs: path_is_allowed only matches exact file paths.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd/phases.rs: allowed paths are assembled for plan-only enforcement, but directory ignores are not supported.
plan: Update path_is_allowed to treat allowed entries that end with '/' (or are directories) as prefix matches.
Add tests for nested paths under allowed directories.
Update callers (if needed) to pass directory prefixes for cache paths.

tasks.title: Fix prune keep-last logic to handle duplicate IDs (rejected tasks)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/prune.rs: keep_last uses HashSet of task IDs to protect tasks.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: rejected tasks are skipped in duplicate detection, allowing duplicate IDs.
keep_last currently can keep more than N tasks when IDs repeat.
plan: Change keep_last protection to track task indices instead of IDs.
Add tests with duplicate IDs to ensure keep_last respects count.
Verify prune reports remain accurate after change.

tasks.title: Optimize queue backfill_missing_fields to avoid O(n^2) ID lookups
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/mutation.rs: backfill_missing_fields loops all tasks and calls new_task_ids.contains(&task.id.trim().to_string()), which is O(n) per task and allocates on every iteration.
plan: Convert new_task_ids into a HashSet<&str> (or HashSet<String>) once before the loop.
Avoid per-task string allocation by comparing trimmed &str where possible.
Add/adjust tests to cover backfill behavior with large task sets or duplicate IDs.

tasks.title: Fix burndown chart for zero-task windows and zero counts
evidence: crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: print_burndown uses bar_len.max(1) so zero counts still show a bar.
crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: max_count can be 0, leading to divide-by-zero/NaN in bar_len calculation.
plan: Handle max_count == 0 with a clear message and early return.
Remove bar_len.max(1) so zero counts render as empty bars.
Add tests for empty/zero-task burndown output.

tasks.title: Add JSON output option for queue stats/history/burndown
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: QueueStatsArgs/QueueHistoryArgs/QueueBurndownArgs have no format option.
crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: report functions print text-only output.
plan: Add --format (text|json) flags to stats/history/burndown CLI args.
Implement JSON structs/serialization in reports.rs.
Update docs and tests to cover JSON output.

tasks.title: Normalize tag casing in stats tag breakdown
evidence: crates/ralph/src/reports.rs: print_stats lowercases tags for filtering but counts tags using original casing.
plan: Normalize tags to lower-case when aggregating tag_counts.
Update any tests that assert tag breakdown output.
Ensure output remains stable for mixed-case tags.

tasks.title: Add module docstrings to integration tests missing file headers
evidence: crates/ralph/tests/*.rs: 19 integration test files start without a module doc comment (e.g., crates/ralph/tests/doctor_contract_test.rs begins with `use ...`), violating the repo docstring requirement.
~/.codex/AGENTS.md: all code files must include a docstring explaining purpose.
plan: Add a concise `//!` module-level docstring to each listed test file describing the test suite purpose.
Keep docstrings consistent in tone and scope across all test files.
Run the existing test suite to ensure the doc comment additions are non-breaking.

tasks.title: Decompose runner execution module into focused submodules
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runner/execution.rs: 1501 lines (wc -l), combining process execution, streaming parsing, and response extraction.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution.rs: contains in-file tests (`#[cfg(test)]` at line ~1177).
plan: Identify cohesive concerns (process spawn, stdout/stderr parsing, assistant response extraction).
Split into submodules (e.g., execution/process.rs, execution/stream.rs, execution/parse.rs) and re-export public APIs.
Move tests into a dedicated test module or integration test file and update imports.

tasks.title: Split run_cmd/phases.rs into per-phase modules
evidence: crates/ralph/src/run_cmd/phases.rs: 1127 lines (wc -l) covering Phase 1/2/3 orchestration and helper logic.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd/phases.rs: in-file test module (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`) with many #[test] cases around line ~700.
plan: Extract phase-specific logic into modules (phase1.rs, phase2.rs, phase3.rs) under run_cmd/.
Move shared helpers into a dedicated module to avoid duplication.
Relocate tests into run_cmd/phases_tests.rs or crates/ralph/tests/run_cmd_phases_test.rs and update imports.

tasks.title: Allow rejected dependencies to unblock runnable tasks (or update docs/tests to match)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: StatusArg::Rejected doc comment says dependents can proceed.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/query.rs: are_dependencies_met only treats TaskStatus::Done as satisfied.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/tests.rs: test_next_runnable_task_skips_rejected_dep asserts rejected dependency blocks.
plan: Decide final dependency policy for rejected tasks (treat rejected as satisfied and undesired by the project owner).
Update are_dependencies_met and runnable selection logic to match the policy.
Adjust tests/docs to match the new behavior and run queue tests.

tasks.title: Move run_cmd.rs tests into a dedicated test module
evidence: crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs: 470 lines (wc -l) with the command router plus in-file tests.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs: `#[cfg(test)]` block at line ~469.
plan: Move run_cmd tests into a separate test module/file.
Keep run_cmd.rs focused on command parsing and dispatch.
Update any imports and ensure cargo test still covers the command router.

tasks.title: Move runutil.rs tests into a dedicated test module
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil.rs: 539 lines (wc -l) with runtime helpers plus in-file tests.
crates/ralph/src/runutil.rs: `#[cfg(test)]` block at line ~381.
plan: Extract runutil tests into a dedicated test module/file.
Retain only production helpers in runutil.rs.
Adjust module visibility as needed to keep tests compiling.

tasks.title: Refactor queue CLI module into subcommand files
evidence: crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs: 699 lines (wc -l) defining multiple queue subcommands and their help text in a single module.
plan: Split queue CLI subcommands into separate modules (e.g., queue/search.rs, queue/stats.rs, queue/repair.rs).
Re-export a small facade in queue/mod.rs to keep CLI wiring clear.
Move or adjust tests to match the new module structure.

tasks.title: Move queue validation tests out of validation.rs
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: 560 lines (wc -l) with both validation logic and in-file tests.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: `#[cfg(test)]` block at line ~363.
plan: Extract validation tests into a dedicated test module or file.
Keep validation helpers in validation.rs and avoid test-only dependencies in production builds.
Update module wiring so tests are still compiled under #[cfg(test)].

tasks.title: Split queue operation tests into smaller modules
evidence: crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/tests.rs: 1078 lines (wc -l) covering many queue operations in one file.
plan: Group tests by operation (archive, status, mutation, query) into separate test modules.
Extract shared fixtures/helpers into a common test util module.
Update mod.rs to include the new test modules under #[cfg(test)].

tasks.title: Document full `ralph queue` subcommands in docs/cli.md
evidence: docs/cli.md queue section mentions validate/list/search only.
crates/ralph/src/cli/queue.rs defines prune/repair/unlock/sort/stats/history/burndown commands.
plan: Add sections for prune/repair/unlock/sort/stats/history/burndown with brief descriptions.
Include examples aligned with CLI help output.
Review docs/cli.md for any other missing queue flags.

evidence: docs/cli.md run section does not mention --include-draft.
crates/ralph/src/cli/run.rs help/examples include --include-draft.
plan: Add --include-draft description to run documentation.
Add example commands for run one/loop with draft inclusion.
Ensure docs match CLI help wording.

evidence: docs/cli.md describes --force only for stale locks.
crates/ralph/src/run_cmd.rs and crates/ralph/src/scan_cmd.rs pass force to require_clean_repo_ignoring_paths.
plan: Update docs/cli.md to note --force bypasses clean-repo checks for run/scan.
Consider adding a warning note in CLI help text.
Verify documentation aligns with actual behavior.

tasks.title: Refactor TUI core module to reduce responsibilities

crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs doc header lists many features (queue browsing, editing, execution, loop, archive, palette), indicating multiple responsibilities in one file.

Split into submodules (e.g., state.rs, actions.rs, persistence.rs, palette.rs) and re-export the public API from mod.rs.

tasks.title: Make reordering tasks in the queue easier by adding functionality to the TUI
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/mod.rs: TUI does not have a way to reorder tasks in the queue.
plan: Add a TUI action that allows the user to reorder tasks in the queue.
Add tests covering successful task reordering and validation/error cases for the new TUI action.
Task order is determined top-down from the queue.json file. Move tasks around easily via arrow keys.
